title: ascium japurense
intro: Ascium japurense, also known as Mexican Prickly Poppy, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, offering health benefits like reducing pain and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Ascium japurense typically include the roots and leaves dried and ground into a powder, or made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
